g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the data and universe selection layer for a quant trading system')
g.text: Survivorship-bias-free universe construction · point-in-time data only
Liquidity filter: min ADV, min price · corporate action adjustments (splits, dividends)
Data quality checks: stale prices, missing bars · halt detection · no AI models used here
Add first — silent bugs here corrupt every downstream layer and every backtest

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the text feature branch using FinBERT')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the market feature branch and LSTM')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the context feature branch for macro and fundamentals')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about Hidden Markov Models for regime detection in quant trading')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about using XGBoost as the predictive model in a quant system')
g.text: Primary: XGBoost · alt: LightGBM · baseline: Random Forest
Alt forecasters: TFT, N-BEATS, N-HiTS (validate vs XGBoost first)
Target: sector-neutralized forward return — not raw price
Calibration: Platt scaling or isotonic regression
Monitoring: SHAP values for feature importance and drift
Train separate model per regime (fed from upstream HMM)
Output: expected return score or calibrated probability

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about walk-forward backtesting for a quant trading system')
g.text: Train T0→T1, test T1→T2, slide forward
True holdout set — never tune on it

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How should I use paper trading before going live with a quant system?')
g.text: Run shadow portfolio on Alpaca paper account · must pass before live capital · min 4–8 weeks

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the portfolio decision layer using cvxpy and contextual bandits')
g.text: Start with: cvxpy constrained mean-variance optimizer (XGBoost return + historical covariance)
Model: contextual bandit — best for daily stock universe selection, do before RL
Future model: RL agent (PPO / SAC via stable-baselines3) — defer until layers 0–4 stable
Add turnover penalty in optimizer objective — smooths rebalancing, cuts transaction costs
Outputs: target weights · target dollar exposure · rebalance decisions

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the risk engine layer for a quant trading system')
g.text: Layer 4 — Risk engine (hard rules, no AI)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the execution engine layer for a quant trading system using Alpaca')
g.text: Convert weights to orders · choose order types · place / monitor / cancel / retry
Reconcile fills with Alpaca account state · slippage estimation before placement
Fill quality tracking — feed actual vs. expected fills back to Layer 3 & 4
No AI models here — intentionally a pure rule-based layer

svg.text: NLP / text: FinBERT · Sentence Transformers · BERTopic · LDA
Time series: LSTM / GRU (optional) · TFT · N-BEATS · N-HiTS
Signal / tabular: XGBoost (primary) · LightGBM (alt) · Random Forest (baseline)
Portfolio: Contextual bandit (stock selection) · cvxpy optimizer
Execution (future): PPO / SAC via stable-baselines3 (RL — defer)
Walk-forward validation only · SHAP for monitoring · paper trade before going live
Sharpe > 2.5 in backtest = likely data leak · IC > 0.05 meaningful, > 0.10 strong
